tldr: Move from Basis Theory's portable card vault to PaymentsOS's full enterprise orchestration platform with smart routing.
why_switch: Need full multi-PSP routing, retry, and failover instead of building it on top of a vault
Want unified analytics and reconciliation across PSPs out of the box
Prefer a battle-tested enterprise orchestrator backed by PayU
Reduce internal engineering burden of running an orchestration layer
Consolidate tokenization, routing, and reporting into one vendor
what_to_export: PSP configurations and credentials currently routed through Basis Theory
Encryption keys and key rotation history (per Basis Theory guidance)
Integration points (services that call the vault)
export_method: Use Basis Theory's Tokens API and management console to enumerate tokens, metadata, and proxy/reactor definitions. Token values themselves cannot be exported in cleartext — coordinate a PCI-compliant vault-to-vault migration with both Basis Theory and PaymentsOS support. Archive logs and configs as JSON. Confirm the exact migration handshake with Basis Theory's official docs/support.
import_method: Engage PaymentsOS sales/onboarding to provision your account and connect your PSPs. Import the vault via the agreed processor-to-processor transfer; do not move raw PANs yourself. Re-create routing rules, retry policies, and webhook subscriptions in PaymentsOS. Re-point your application to PaymentsOS APIs and dual-run with Basis Theory for at least one billing cycle. Verify endpoints and resource shapes against PaymentsOS official docs.

concept_mapping.note: Vault-to-vault transfer required, not API copy
PaymentsOS handles routing natively; reactors may not be needed
Most PSP connections are first-class in PaymentsOS
Replace bespoke routing logic with declarative rules
Get a written PaymentsOS quote — pricing is not public

gotchas: Vault-to-vault transfers are PCI-sensitive and require coordination with both vendors
PaymentsOS sales cycle is enterprise-only — onboarding takes time
PaymentsOS developer experience reportedly trails newer entrants; budget extra integration time
You will lose the option to swap orchestrators trivially — orchestration logic becomes vendor-managed
Re-implementing routing rules requires careful parity testing against current behavior
Confirm SLAs, data residency, and PCI scope responsibilities before committing

email.subject: Migration plan: Basis Theory to PaymentsOS
email.body:
Hi team,

I want to move from Basis Theory to PaymentsOS. Today we use Basis Theory as the vault and built our own orchestration on top — that's not a great use of engineering time. PaymentsOS gives us routing, retry, failover, and reporting as a product.

Plan: enumerate tokens and configs in Basis Theory, coordinate a PCI-compliant vault-to-vault transfer with both vendors, recreate routing rules in PaymentsOS, dual-run for a billing cycle, then cut over. Estimated 4-10 weeks.

Things I want to confirm before commit: written enterprise quote from PaymentsOS, SLA terms, PCI scope responsibilities, and a hands-on dev review since their DX reputedly trails newer orchestrators.

Thoughts?
